reasoning: Following a June 5, 2025 phone call with Chinese President Xi Jinping, Donald Trump announced he accepted an invitation to visit China. However, as of the June 15, 2025 knowledge cutoff, no date has been scheduled. Furthermore, Trump is attending the G7 Summit in Kananaskis, Canada, from June 15 to 17. Presidential state visits to China require extensive logistical preparation and advanced diplomatic coordination (lower-level teams just began meeting in London the week of June 9). It is highly improbable that a full presidential visit could be planned, announced, and executed in the mere 13 days remaining between the end of the G7 and the July 1 deadline. Therefore, the chance of this occurring before July 1, 2025, is extremely slim.
